sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# Let me try moving the green item down and left from the current state
# First, let me check the current green item position
seg = current_frame.segmentation
for n in seg['nodes']:
    if n['color'] == 'N':
        b = n['boundary']
        min_r = min(p[0] for p in b)
        max_r = max(p[0] for p in b)
        min_c = min(p[1] for p in b)
        max_c = max(p[1] for p in b)
        print(f"Green item: rows={min_r}-{max_r} cols={min_c}-{max_c}")

# Let me also check the gray box position
for n in seg['nodes']:
    if n['color'] == 'g':
        b = n['boundary']
        min_r = min(p[0] for p in b)
        max_r = max(p[0] for p in b)
        min_c = min(p[1] for p in b)
        max_c = max(p[1] for p in b)
        if min_r >= 28 and min_r <= 32:  # The gray box containing the green item
            print(f"Gray box: rows={min_r}-{max_r} cols={min_c}-{max_c}")

# Try moving down and left
result = action(['DOWN', 'LEFT'])
print("\nAfter DOWN, LEFT:", last_action_result)

# Check green item position after
seg = current_frame.segmentation
for n in seg['nodes']:
    if n['color'] == 'N':
        b = n['boundary']
        min_r = min(p[0] for p in b)
        max_r = max(p[0] for p in b)
        min_c = min(p[1] for p in b)
        max_c = max(p[1] for p in b)
        print(f"Green item: rows={min_r}-{max_r} cols={min_c}-{max_c}")
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>
